content.problemStatement:
In the magic land, there is an annual parade hold on each spring.




There are **N** cities in magic land, and **M** directed roads between cities.


On the parade, there will be some(may be 0) heroes travel in this land, for each hero:
He start at city begin[i], traveling to some cities, and finish at city end[i]. Note that: begin[i] may be equals to end[i], but he must at least moved to another city during this travel. He can go on one road many times, but it will have a cost for each time.




The cost of this parade is the sum of these items:


1. The sum of costs by traveling on roads. (If a road is passed by k heroes, then it must be count k times.)


2. If for a hero, he ended at a city that not equals to his start city, i.e. begin[i] != end[i], then it will cost **C** dollars to move him back to his home.


3. If for a city, there is no heroes visited, then we must pay for the citizen **C** dollars as compensate.




The value of **C** may change every year, and we can predict this value in the following **K** years. 
Your task is: calculate the minimal cost of each year.
content.inputFormat: First line contains three integers: N, M, and K, representing the number of cities, the number of directed roads, and the number of years, respectively. The next M lines contain three integers each: S[i], T[i], and V[i], describing a directed road from S[i] to T[i] with a cost of V[i] dollars. The following K lines contain an integer each: C[i], describing the value of C in that year.
content.outputFormat: Output K lines, each containing an integer corresponding to the minimal cost of each year.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The minimal cost for each year is calculated by summing the costs of the shortest paths for each hero and adding the costs of moving the heroes back to their home cities if necessary, as well as the costs of compensating the citizens in cities that were not visited.

The minimal cost for each year is calculated by summing the costs of the shortest paths for each hero and adding the costs of moving the heroes back to their home cities if necessary, as well as the costs of compensating the citizens in cities that were not visited.

The minimal cost for each year is calculated by summing the costs of the shortest paths for each hero and adding the costs of moving the heroes back to their home cities if necessary, as well as the costs of compensating the citizens in cities that were not visited.

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we first need to find the shortest path between each pair of cities using the Floyd-Warshall algorithm. Then, for each year, we calculate the minimal cost by summing the costs of the shortest paths for each hero and adding the costs of moving the heroes back to their home cities if necessary, as well as the costs of compensating the citizens in cities that were not visited.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N^3 + M + K) per test case, O(T * (N^3 + M + K)) total. The Floyd-Warshall algorithm takes O(N^3) time, and we need to iterate over all roads and years.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N^2 + M + K) — the dominant structure is the distance matrix.
